title: Branch naming + PR body + merge + force-push policy
description: Formal shape of git-conventions.yaml — one entry per git-surface convention (branch-naming, pr-body, merge-mechanics, force-push, base-branch). Each entry carries a template / pattern, validations tagged with severities, and prose. Top-level cross-cuts: `branch_slug_derivation` (the 6-step procedure), `multi_issue_pr_rules` (when allowed, what's checked), and `integration_branches` (the opt-in long-running shared branch construct). PR title is handled by titles.yaml's `pr` entry — this schema does not duplicate it. Consumed by the PR-open path of the create-issue / transition-state skills. Companion per COR-018.

conventions.description: Convention entries keyed by surface id (e.g., `branch-name`, `pr-body`, `merge`, `force-push`, `base-branch`).

steps.description: Ordered transformations applied to the issue title to produce the branch slug.

allowed.description: Whether multi-issue PRs are permitted. True (exception path); single-issue is the default.

opt_in.description: Whether integration branches require explicit owner designation (true).

owner_types.description: Issue types that may own an integration branch (EPIC, Feature, Umbrella — any container).

branch_name_pattern.description: Regex the integration branch name must match.

designation_marker_pattern.description: Regex the body's `Integration:` line must match. Capturing the slug.

designation_position.description: Where the marker sits — first line of the owning issue's body, above the parent-ref. Matches the integration_marker block in body-format.yaml.

propagation_rule.description: How descendants inherit the marker. The agent stamps the same line on every descendant body on designation; later filings under the owning issue inherit at creation.

closure_path.description: How the owning issue and integration branch close. Marked descendants close on merge into the integration branch via `Closes #N`. Owning issue closes via a final `integration/<slug>` → main PR with `Closes #<owning>` — squash-merged with --delete-branch.

force_push_allowed.description: Whether force-push is allowed on the integration branch. False — same rule as `main` (shared branch).

surface_kind.description: Which git surface this convention governs.

pattern.description: Regex or template the surface must match. Null when the convention is prose-only (e.g., force-push policy).

name.description: Optional machine-readable rule identifier (e.g. 'body.placeholder.empty-checkbox-section'). Used by DEC-031's declare-in-schema / enforce-in-code pattern.
